name: Buoyancy
description: Objects and characters react to fluid volumes by floating, sinking, bobbing, or resisting motion based on submerged state.
design_notes: Buoyancy works best when fluid_volume_id, buoyancy sample points, drag coefficients, and wake/sleep rules are explicit. The mechanic should define which bodies float, which sink, how players control submerged motion, and how waterline transitions affect audio, camera, and collision.
design_purpose: Make water, lava, gas, or other fluid spaces feel physically distinct from dry ground.
Support traversal, object puzzles, survival hazards, and vehicle handling through submerged motion.
Give designers a way to classify objects by density, drag, and float behavior.
player_fantasy: The player feels the resistance and lift of fluid spaces and can predict which objects will float or sink.

parameters.name: fluid_volume_id
parameters.description: Stable identifier for the volume that defines surface height, density, drag, and damage rules.

parameters.name: buoyancy_sample_points
parameters.description: Number of body-local points checked against the fluid surface to estimate submerged ratio.

parameters.name: displaced_volume_multiplier
parameters.description: Multiplier that controls how strongly the body rises relative to its mass and submerged ratio.

parameters.name: drag_coefficient
parameters.description: Resistance applied to movement while submerged, separated from dry-ground friction or air drag.

parameters.name: surface_snap_tolerance
parameters.description: Small tolerance used to reduce jitter when a body rests near the fluid surface.

edge_cases: An object is partly inside two overlapping fluid volumes with different density or damage rules.
A sleeping rigidbody enters fluid due to moving water, streaming, or a rising flood volume.
A player exits the water while the camera, audio filter, and movement controller still think they are submerged.
Network clients simulate bobbing locally but the server resolves a different waterline contact.
common_bugs: Buoyancy pushes upward from the center of mass only, causing long objects to float unrealistically or flip violently.
Drag is applied every render frame instead of fixed step and changes with frame rate.
Submerged state toggles rapidly at the waterline, spamming splash effects and movement mode changes.
Save/load restores object position but not sleeping state, submerged ratio, or fluid_volume_id.
balancing_notes: High drag makes water feel heavy and readable but can frustrate traversal if the player has little control authority.
Puzzle objects should have visually learnable density classes so players do not need trial and error for every item.
Surface bobbing can add life, but too much motion makes aiming, platforming, or item placement uncomfortable.
accessibility_notes: Provide stable camera options and reduced bobbing for players sensitive to waterline motion.
Use audio, particle, and UI state changes in addition to color shifts for submerged or drowning states.

implementation_notes.unity: Use trigger volumes for fluid_volume_id and sample body-local buoyancy points against a surface query. Apply acceleration and drag in FixedUpdate, wake sleeping bodies when flood state changes, and replicate authoritative submerged state for boats or multiplayer objects.
implementation_notes.godot: Area2D or Area3D can mark fluid volumes, while RigidBody integration applies lift from sampled points. Keep submerged_ratio, fluid_volume_id, and sleeping state in save data, and debug draw sample points plus surface intersections during tuning.
implementation_notes.unreal: Physics Volumes, Water plugin data, or custom components can provide fluid surfaces. Apply per-body lift from sample sockets, clamp angular impulses for stability, and let the server own buoyant vehicles or puzzle objects when replication matters.
implementation_notes.web: For Matter.js, Phaser, or custom solvers, compute submerged ratio from sample points and apply lift plus drag in the fixed simulation pass. Pool splash events and avoid allocating per-point arrays each frame in large water scenes.

example_games.title: Subnautica
example_games.note: Uses underwater movement and vehicle behavior as core exploration systems.

example_games.title: Half-Life 2
example_games.note: Uses floating objects in water as part of physics interaction.
